narratives.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narratives.reasoning: This direct quote provides a severe and derogatory criticism of Western (specifically American) society, labeling them as 'pigs' and 'trash', which aligns with the narrative of discrediting the West by attacking its character and moral standing.

narratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narratives.reasoning: This statement explicitly questions the reliability and framing of Western media coverage, accusing it of prolonging the war, which directly fits the narrative of undermining confidence in media sources.

narratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narratives.reasoning: This quote explicitly evokes fear and anxiety about the potential for nuclear war, presenting it as an imminent or desired outcome, which aligns with the narrative of amplifying war-related fears.

narratives.evidence_quote: Russia is Winning, Nuclear Threat is a Sign of Desperation & Very Serious
narratives.reasoning: The title of the post makes a direct prediction about the outcome of the war ('Russia is Winning') and speculates on the meaning and seriousness of the nuclear threat, fitting the definition of speculating on war outcomes.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narratives.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narratives.reasoning: This statement highlights negative outcomes and moral failings attributed to Western governance structures, predicting or describing adverse consequences stemming from their actions.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is overreacting
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly claims that the US (and by extension the West) consistently and disproportionately exaggerates threats from Russia and China, matching the definition of presenting the West's reaction as disproportionate.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
subnarratives.reasoning: This implies the West, through its media, is deliberately prolonging the war for its own reasons (e.g., to weaken Russia), disregarding the resulting Ukrainian deaths, which aligns with the subnarrative that the West is only interested in its own benefits.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: The statement suggests that the current Western approach (media framing) is actively preventing successful negotiations from ending the war, thereby discrediting the potential of diplomatic efforts.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: I do not think an amerikan life is worth 1/2 as much as an African (or any other life outside the west) ..they are trash ..not worth the effort ..get to a point of “kill them all” either because they do not care and they want us all dead ..or because they just sit back and “DO NOT CARE”

This extreme dehumanization and condemnation is a clear attack on Western values and populace but does not fit any of the more specific diplomatic subnarratives provided.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Distrust towards Media: Western media is an instrument of propaganda

subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly labels Western media as 'corporation propaganda,' framing it as a tool that withholds or distorts information, which aligns with the definition of discrediting Western media institutions as instruments of propaganda.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly evokes fear and anxiety about the use of nuclear weapons, framing a full nuclear war as a plausible and imminent outcome, which aligns with the subnarrative definition.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: By continuing the war we risk WWIII
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement implies that the current Western approach to the war (prolonging it) is leading to more death and potentially escalating the conflict, which evokes fear of a broader, more catastrophic war.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: I am sure Scott will have a safe place he can go .. he talks the most sense ….pity he cannot all but talk about it….most of the rest of the “un Nuclear war countries” will have to survive the horrors left behind of Russia China`Britain (lots of europe) amerika canada australia NZ..
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote amplifies war-related fears by describing a post-conflict scenario of 'horrors left behind' for most of the world, supporting the parent narrative of amplifying fear but not fitting neatly into the more specific subnarrative definitions provided.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Speculating war outcomes: Ukrainian army is collapsing
subnarratives.reasoning: The title directly states that Russia is winning, which implies the opposing force, the Ukrainian army, is losing or collapsing in its ability to resist effectively.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going? Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote speculates on the potential future actions (resorting to nuclear weapons) and motivations (desperation) of a party in the war, which fits the parent narrative of speculating on war outcomes but does not align with the specific definitions about army collapse or loss of territory.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Sanctions imposed by Western countries will backfire
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing …
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote argues that Western (specifically American) demonization and aggressive policy towards Russia is based on a false premise and is disproportionate, implying that the West's confrontational stance is misguided and will lead to negative consequences stemming from its own actions.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over …thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
